channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Faith and Hope in a Turbulent World: Lessons from a Pastor and the Cricket World Cup
item.description: Justin Langer reflects on a chance encounter with George, a Ghanaian pastor, during a trip to Manchester for the Cricket World Cup. Their conversation about faith, hope, and love highlights the univer...

item.title: Parliament to Question School Principals on Capitation Fund Management
item.description: Hundreds of public secondary school principals in Kenya are set to appear before parliament as lawmakers intensify scrutiny over the management of billions of shillings allocated to schools. The Natio...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Defends Transparency Efforts Amid China Disputes
item.description: Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela, spokesperson of the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) for the West Philippine Sea, rejected Senator Robin Padilla's claim that the government's transparency campaign in the area ...

item.title: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller: Pioneering Nun and First Woman to Earn Computer Science Doctorate
item.description: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller (born Evelyn Marie Keller, December 17, 1913 – January 10, 1985) was a pioneering American Catholic nun, educator, and computer scientist. She became the first woman in the ...

item.title: Springboks Tour Squad Finalized Without Richie Mo'unga
item.description: New Zealand head coach Dave Rennie has confirmed that Richie Mo'unga will not be part of the Springboks tour squad for the 'Greatest Rivalry' series against South Africa. The decision follows Mo'unga'...

item.title: India's First Hydrogen-Powered Train Launched in Haryana
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched India's first hydrogen-powered fuel cell train from Jind in Haryana, marking a significant step towards sustainable rail transport. The train, part of a global sh...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke and Its Impact on Brain Health: A Growing Health Concern
item.description: Wildfire smoke poses significant risks to brain health beyond its well-known effects on the respiratory and cardiovascular systems. Research indicates that fine particulate matter (PM2.5) from wildfir...

item.title: Pakistan leads SCO border security discussions with member states
item.description: Pakistan chaired and hosted the 12th meeting of the Heads of Border Services of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) on Friday. The event brought together representatives from Belarus, India, I...

item.title: Two SANDF members trapped in abandoned mine shaft during anti-illegal mining operation
item.description: Two members of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) are currently trapped in an abandoned vertical mine shaft following an incident during Operation Prosper, an anti-illegal mining operati...

item.title: KUCCPS Offers Late Application Chance for Over 57,000 KCSE Candidates Who Missed University Placements
item.description: The Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has launched a late application window for degree programmes, targeting thousands of KCSE candidates from 2022 to 2025 who qualif...

item.title: Government to adjust fuel prices daily amid US-Iran tensions in Persian Gulf
item.description: Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik announced that fuel prices will be fixed daily due to fluctuations in international market prices caused by renewed hostilities between Iran and the US. Addressing...

item.title: Student Compensation After Catfishing Incident
item.description: A 19-year-old woman, Sasha-Jay Davies from Aberdare, has received £10,000 in compensation after her identity was stolen by Elha Mai Weston, who created fake dating profiles on platforms like Tinder, T...

item.title: UNESCO and Kakama launch Swahili disaster preparedness guide for media institutions
item.description: UNESCO and the East African Kiswahili Commission (Kakama) have launched a Disaster Preparedness and Response Framework for Media Institutions to strengthen the role of news organizations in disaster r...

item.title: P680K Shabu Seized During BARMM Gun Ban Enforcement
item.description: On the first day of enforcing the gun ban in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region of Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), Philippine law enforcement agencies conducted a successful operation that resulted in the seiz...

item.title: ADC Calls for Investigation into Tinubu's Leadership and Control Over Administration
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has demanded that the National Assembly investigate whether President Bola Ahmed Tinubu remains fit to carry out his duties, citing controversies over leadership ...

item.title: Strategies for Securing Pre-Seed Funding Without a Product: Insights from Disrupt 2026 Panel
item.description: The article discusses the challenges faced by pre-seed founders in securing funding amid the surge of AI startups dominating seed investments. At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, a panel titled 'Winning Pre-S...

item.title: Sydney Roosters Face Melbourne Storm in NRL Round 20
item.description: The Sydney Roosters will take on the Melbourne Storm in a highly anticipated NRL Premiership clash during Round 20 of the 2026 season at Allianz Stadium in Sydney. The match, scheduled for July 17, 20...

item.title: Federal Rule Limits Foreign Student Visas to Four Years, Impacting California Campuses
item.description: A new federal rule limits most international student stays in the U.S. to four years, ending the previous policy that allowed students to remain for the duration of their degree. This change, effectiv...

item.title: Spain's Yamal and Porro Train Separately Ahead of World Cup Final Amid Injury Concerns
item.description: Spain's Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro trained separately from the rest of the squad ahead of the FIFA World Cup final against Argentina, as reported by Reuters. The duo, who are key players for Spain, ...

item.title: Canberra United's New Owners Focus on Training Facilities
item.description: The article details the new ownership of Canberra United, an A-League Women's team, and their plans to establish a dedicated training facility. The Australian Sports Group (ASG), led by chairman Morri...
